blocks.text: DTC U0122: F-CAN communication error (PGM-FI ECU-VSA modulator control unit communication failure)

Inter-control unit communication (F-CAN) for powertrain/chassis systems uses two communication lines (F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L) to send and receive multiple signals simultaneously between multiple control units. If the PGM-FI ECU continues for more than a set time with no information sent from the VSA modulator control unit by F-CAN, or if it continues for more than a set number of times with abnormal information sent from the VSA modulator control unit, the PGM-FI ECU will determines that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
If any of the following is established

items.text: When the PGM-FI ECU cannot receive signals from the VSA Modulator Control Unit sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L for more than 1 second.
When the PGM-FI ECU receives an error signal from the VSA modulator control unit sent using F-CAN_H and F-CAN_L more than 20 times continuously.
Disconnection of F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_H line)
Disconnection of F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_L line)
Ground fault in F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_H line)
Ground fault in F-CAN circuit code (F-CAN_L line)
VSA modulator Control unit failure (including blown or blown fuse)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
